methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $400.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Palantir
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $400B+

private.name: Liquid AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2.35B

private.name: Field AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Twelve Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $250M

data.narrative:
Cortical Labs is a Series A-stage AI & Software company that has raised $11M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 4 direct comparables in the same sector and stage, supplemented by reference to a broader comp set including 1 public company (Palantir, PLTR) and 8 private firms spanning early to mature stages.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $11M raised proxy, implying a $22M valuation. Peer-median analysis uses the median valuation of 4 same-stage, same-sector comparables—Palantir ($400.0B), Liquid AI ($2.4B), Field AI ($2.0B), and Twelve Labs ($250M)—yielding a $2.2B median. The peer range spans $250M to $400.0B, reflecting extreme dispersion driven by Palantir's exceptional scale. The low anchor of the peer set (Twelve Labs at $250M) and the stage-progression method ($22M) both point toward more conservative valuations for an early-stage firm.

The triangulated analysis implies a range of $19M to $2.5B, with a median of $1.1B. This wide range reflects material uncertainty: the peer-median method is heavily influenced by outlier valuations, particularly Palantir, while stage-progression relies on raised capital as a proxy absent disclosed revenue or profitability metrics. Key unknowns include Cortical Labs' revenue traction, customer concentration, and whether its technology and market position justify a premium or discount relative to contemporaries.
